mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is particularly useful for deploying large language models efficiently and reliably in production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the llm-d deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Install the CLI for Linux systems.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in a dedicated namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> This command will remove the llm-d release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up the current configuration, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current configuration.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that the pods are running with the new version.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or the repository may be private.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure you have access to the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the application logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or the endpoint is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get svc -n llm-d' and check the endpoints.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the resource and tool requirements for a successful installation.
